commands.review_summary:
The PR changes model-selection auth-profile override eligibility from a raw provider string check to the existing alias-aware stored-credential compatibility helper.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main compares the stored profile's raw provider against accepted providers, so an anthropic stored credential can be cleared when the run accepts claude-cli. I did not run a live WSL2 claude-cli reproduction.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor-supplied patched-run proof and maintainer auth-provider risk acceptance; automation cannot produce the reporter's real WSL2 claude-cli evidence.

target.title: fix(auth): keep alias-compatible auth-profile overrides instead of clearing them
target.body:
## Summary
- **Problem:** A claude-cli session's stored `authProfileOverride` was cleared whenever the profile's raw credential provider was not literally in the accepted-providers list — e.g. an `anthropic` credential serving a `claude-cli` run. Auto-selection then re-picked a different profile, so the CLI session's auth profile oscillated between `anthropic:claude-cli` and `anthropic:default`.
- **Solution:** Use the alias-aware compatibility check (`isStoredCredentialCompatibleWithAuthProvider`) that `session-override.ts` already uses, so an alias-compatible override is kept rather than cleared.
- **What changed:** the eligibility check in `model-selection.ts` (raw provider-string `includes` → alias-aware compatibility).
- **What did NOT change:** the auto-selection ordering; user-sourced overrides; non-claude-cli providers.

## Motivation
Each profile flip mismatched the persisted CLI session binding's `authProfileId`, invalidating the session (`invalidatedReason: "auth-profile"`), forcing a fresh `claude` session and losing conversation continuity — a self-inflicted, recurring history loss.

## Change Type
- [x] Bug fix

## Scope
- [x] Auth (auth-profile selection)

## Linked Issue/PR
Related to claude-cli session invalidation / history loss. Checked: fixes a bug/regression.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior addressed:** auth-profile override oscillating between two alias-compatible anthropic profiles across runs of the same session.
- **Real environment:** OpenClaw on WSL2, claude-cli backend.
- **Evidence:** a live session registry entry showed `authProfileOverride: "anthropic:default"` / `authProfileOverrideSource: "auto"` with `cliSessionBindings.claude-cli.authProfileId: "anthropic:default"`, while `auth-state.json` `lastGood.anthropic` was `anthropic:claude-cli` — the two writers disagreeing.
- **What was NOT tested:** a live patched-build run (build green).

## Root Cause
`model-selection.ts` checked the profile's raw credential `provider` against `acceptedAuthProviders` with a plain string `includes`, while `session-override.ts` uses alias-aware `isStoredCredentialCompatibleWithAuthProvider`. For a `claude-cli` run, `acceptedAuthProviders` is `["claude-cli"]`, so an `anthropic`-provider credential was judged ineligible and the override deleted — then re-picked differently next turn.

## Regression Test Plan
`src/auto-reply/reply/model-selection.test.ts` / `session-override.test.ts`: assert a `claude-cli` selection does not clear an alias-compatible `anthropic:default` override. 203 tests pass across the touched files (no regressions in model-selection / session-override suites).

## User-visible / Behavior Changes
None directly visible; eliminates spurious CLI session resets.

## Diagram
```
Before: claude-cli run + anthropic:default override -> raw check fails -> override CLEARED -> re-pick -> flap
After:  claude-cli run + anthropic:default override -> alias check passes -> override KEPT -> stable
```

## Security Impact
- New permissions: No
- New secrets handling: No
- New network calls: No
- New exec surface: No
- New data access: No (uses an existing, already-imported compatibility helper)

## Repro + Verification
- **Environment:** claude-cli session whose auth override is an alias-compatible non-`claude-cli` profile.
- **Steps:** run consecutive turns / a `/model` directive turn.
- **Expected:** override stays pinned.
- **Actual (before):** override cleared and re-picked, invalidating the CLI session.

## Evidence
- [x] Failing-then-passing regression tests

## Human Verification
Verified: model-selection and session-override test suites pass; `pnpm build` green; root cause confirmed against a live session registry + `auth-state.json`. NOT verified: live patched-build run.

## Review Conversations
- [x] Will resolve bot review threads before merge

## Compatibility / Migration
Backward compatible. No config or migration.

## Risks and Mitigations
- The check now keeps more overrides — but only those genuinely alias-compatible per the same helper `session-override.ts` already trusts; consistency between the two call sites is the goal. Low risk.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
